title: Enabling support for --log-opt max-size=xx for having command line option for passing max size of log files
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *pmoogi-redhat* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign **luap99** after the PR has been reviewed. You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @luap99` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
need rebase, and your git commit messages should include yout sign.
@pmoogi-redhat thanks for the PR!  Our CI requires a signature for each PR.  Please do `git commit --amend -s` and then fetch, rebase, and push the PR again.

@pmoogi-redhat I'm not seeing the gofmt error quickly in the logs, but to fix that use the following command on each of your go files, it's likely you have a rogue space somewhere:  `gofmt -s -w mygofile.go`  then do  `git add file(s)`, `git commit --amend -s`, fetch, rebase and push.

@giuseppe could you please review updated version and give approval for it towards PR ? I have reflected all changes and did validation on my workspace.  make validation is not still working locally and you just suggested that it is fine to be resubmitted as PR as CI will take of it.

> @pmoogi-redhat thanks for the PR! Our CI requires a signature for each PR. Please do `git commit --amend -s` and then fetch, rebase, and push the PR again.

Done
you need fetch upstream and rebase to resolve the `Conflicting files`

git rebase -i origin 
Then squash all of your commits down into one PR 
git push --force
Should clean this up.
@rhatdan kindly see if changes can be taken in as cleaned up, rebased on top of upstream/master latest. thank you.
I would like to reexamine what this PR is attempting to solve.  From reading the podman code, it looks like this is currently handled in pkg/specgen.  So I am not sure what is broken?

> I would like to reexamine what this PR is attempting to solve. From reading the podman code, it looks like this is currently handled in pkg/specgen. So I am not sure what is broken?

@rhatdan when i forked out master version and applied custom built in my local system, i found --log-opt max-size was not supported. that was the reason for my PR almost more than a week ago. I reflected all review comments and tried to rebase and push my change.. not sure when pkg/spec/parse.go, createconfig.go got dropped out from upstream version.  Let me know if support for --log-opt max-size already exists in latest repo version.

I have not tested but it looks like the code is there:

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/cmd/podman/common/specgen.go#L603
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/pkg/specgen/generate/container_create.go#L290

Could you verify that this is not working?

I have created a couple of containers and am seeing the log-size-max flag passed to conmon.

$ podman run -d --log-opt max-size=10k alpine sleep 100
$ ps -auxww | grep conmon
dwalsh    354191  0.0  0.0  81264  1916 ?        Ssl  09:42   0:00 /usr/bin/conmon --api-version 1 -c 7379c4900091b94f75bcb5bd53fe46a3384f58df7eba1d85a6a7af301845f5dc -u 7379c4900091b94f75bcb5bd53fe46a3384f58df7eba1d85a6a7af301845f5dc -r /usr/bin/crun -b /home/dwalsh/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/7379c4900091b94f75bcb5bd53fe46a3384f58df7eba1d85a6a7af301845f5dc/userdata -p /run/user/3267/containers/overlay-containers/7379c4900091b94f75bcb5bd53fe46a3384f58df7eba1d85a6a7af301845f5dc/userdata/pidfile -n quirky_shannon --exit-dir /run/user/3267/libpod/tmp/exits --socket-dir-path /run/user/3267/libpod/tmp/socket -s -l k8s-file:/home/dwalsh/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/7379c4900091b94f75bcb5bd53fe46a3384f58df7eba1d85a6a7af301845f5dc/userdata/ctr.log --log-level error **--log-size-max 10000** --runtime-arg --log-format=json --runtime-arg --log --runtime-arg=/run/user/3267/containers/overlay-containers/7379c4900091b94f75bcb5bd53fe46a3384f58df7eba1d85a6a7af301845f5dc/userdata/oci-log --conmon-pidfile /run/user/3267/containers/overlay-containers/7379c4900091b94f75bcb5bd53fe46a3384f58df7eba1d85a6a7af301845f5dc/userdata/conmon.pid --exit-command /usr/bin/podman --exit-command-arg --root --exit-command-arg /home/dwalsh/.local/share/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --runroot --exit-command-arg /run/user/3267/containers --exit-command-arg --log-level --exit-command-arg error --exit-command-arg --cgroup-manager --exit-command-arg systemd --exit-command-arg --tmpdir --exit-command-arg /run/user/3267/libpod/tmp --exit-command-arg --runtime --exit-command-arg crun --exit-command-arg --storage-driver --exit-command-arg overlay --exit-command-arg --storage-opt --exit-command-arg overlay.mount_program=/home/dwalsh/bin/fuse-overlayfs --exit-command-arg --events-backend --exit-command-arg journald --exit-command-arg container --exit-command-arg cleanup --exit-command-arg 7379c4900091b94f75bcb5bd53fe46a3384f58df7eba1d85a6a7af301845f5dc

I think this works correctly now. Closing.
I added a PR to make LogSize visiable in container inspect.
